title: Commercial office, Type V-B, stair geometry and guard outside the IBC limits
notes: Tests the new IBC stair-geometry rules: tread 10 in (< 11 in, IBC 1011.5.2), riser 7.5 in (> 7 in, IBC 1011.5.2 — exercises the max_dimension_check), guard 38 in (< 42 in, IBC 1015.3). Each has a legitimate exception the engine can't rule out from a scalar (spiral/winder stairs for tread/riser; stair-side handrail-guards for the 38 in guard), so all three emit needs_review rather than a hard violation. Area/stories/exits/corridor/stair-width are clean so only the three geometry rules fire.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Stories: 2
Building height: 28 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 80
IECC: Title 24 Part 6
Electrical: 200 A main breaker
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 48 in. Corridor width: 60 in (lobby).
Stairway width: 48 in. Tread depth: 10 in. Max riser: 7.5 in. Guard height: 38 in.
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south). EXIT DOOR 36 in each.
WC 4 LAV 4. CALGreen low-flow fixtures. Construction waste management plan.

ground_truth.rationale: 6,000 sf per-story sprinklered B/V-B is within the Table 506.2 allowance
2 stories within the B/V-B sprinklered 3-story limit
Both corridors (48, 60 in) clear the 44 in IBC 1020.3 minimum — clean true negative alongside the geometry violations
Stair is 48 in, clearing the 44 in IBC 1011.2 minimum — clean true negative

Tread depth is 10 in, below the 11 in IBC 1011.5.2 minimum. Spiral/winder/alternating-tread stairs allow narrower treads, which the engine can't rule out from a scalar, so it flags needs_review rather than a hard violation.

Riser height is 7.5 in, over the 7 in IBC 1011.5.2 maximum (exercises the max_dimension_check). Spiral/winder/alternating-tread stairs allow taller risers, so needs_review rather than a hard violation.

Guard height is 38 in, below the 42 in IBC 1015.3 minimum. A stair-side guard whose top rail also serves as a handrail (34-38 in) is a documented exception, so needs_review rather than a hard violation.
